_note: ORIGINAL CS Navigator content added to align Learn with the existing Part 2 quiz category. No source prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Algorithm Problems: Part 2
summary: Coordinate related state and guard unavailable candidates.
refresher: A multi-step Java algorithm may preserve a best value and index or a total and count. Define what each variable means after a pass, including empty input and ties.

blocks.body: A multi-step Java algorithm may preserve a best value and index or a total and count. Define what each variable means after a pass, including empty input and ties. Coordinate related state and guard unavailable candidates.

blocks.caption: The first array item initializes the candidate, and the empty guard prevents invalid access.

blocks.title: Track the state
blocks.body: An empty array has no element zero, so define its result before initializing the candidate from array data.

blocks.why: Index zero has already supplied the current candidate, leaving later elements to compare.
Equality does not replace the stored index when the update condition uses greater-than.
